steps.title: Identify the core research finding
steps.content: The decisive clue is that people perceive meaningful sounds as quieter than meaningless sounds at the same volume. The study found that participants treated nature sounds as meaningless noise, establishing the contrast between how meaningful and meaningless sounds would be perceived.

steps.title: Apply the finding to the specific scenario
steps.content:
With both sounds at 61.5 decibels, the meaningful music would be perceived as quieter while the meaningless nature sounds would be perceived as louder—exactly what choice C describes.

The other choices either imagine unnecessary volume adjustments, confuse perceived loudness with actual volume, or falsely claim both sounds would seem equally meaningful.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
